reasoning: The primary driver of unrest in Mozambique was the disputed late-2024 election. Even at the height of the protests and violence (over 300 reported dead between October 2024 and January 2025), the US State Department only escalated its travel advisory to Level 3 ("Reconsider Travel"). The new president was inaugurated in mid-January 2025, and subsequent reporting from February and March 2025 indicates a general calming of the situation, leading other nations to lift travel warnings and the US Embassy to reportedly downgrade back to a Level 2 advisory by April. Since a Level 4 ("Do Not Travel") advisory requires a catastrophic, nation-wide collapse in security (e.g., active civil war, severe pandemic)—which did not occur even during the peak of recent unrest—the probability of reaching Level 4 before July 2025 is negligible. Only an entirely unexpected new national catastrophe could trigger this within the brief 60-day remaining window.
